2604.18177.title: STaD: Scaffolded Task Design for Identifying Compositional Skill Gaps in LLMs
2604.18177.paper_summary: Benchmarks are often used as a standard to understand LLM capabilities in different domains. However, aggregate benchmark scores provide limited insight into compositional skill gaps of LLMs and how to improve them. To make these weaknesses visible, we propose Scaffolded Task Design (STaD) framework. STaD generates controlled variations of benchmark tasks based on the concept of scaffolding, which introduces structured, incremental support in a step-by-step manner. Rather than inspecting failures individually, this approach enables systematic and scalable probing of model behavior by identifying the specific reasoning skill compositions they lack. Treating the LLM as a black box, our experiments on six models of varying sizes reveal multiple failure points in three reasoning benchmarks and highlight each model's unique and distinct skill gaps.
2604.18177.paper_summary_zh: 基準常被用作理解大型語言模型（LLM）在不同領域能力的標準。<lb/>然而，綜合基準分數對於LLM的組合技能差距及如何改進它們提供的見解有限。<lb/>為了使這些弱點可見，我們提出了支架任務設計（STaD）框架。<lb/>STaD根據支架的概念生成基準任務的受控變化，這種方法以逐步的方式引入結構化的、漸進的支持。<lb/>這種方法不是單獨檢查失敗，而是通過識別它們缺乏的特定推理技能組合，實現對模型行為的系統性和可擴展探測。<lb/>將LLM視為一個黑箱，我們對六個不同大小的模型進行的實驗揭示了三個推理基準中的多個失敗點，並突顯了每個模型獨特且明顯的技能差距。
2604.18177.author: Sungeun An et.al.
2604.18177.authors: Sungeun An, Swanand Ravindra Kadhe, Shailja Thakur, Chad DeLuca, Hima Patel
